name: Cream Cheese Apple Cake
recipeInstructions:
CAKE: Preheat oven to 350°F. Tightly wrap the outside of a 9-inch springform pan with foil so it covers the bottom and sides of the pan. Butter the inside of the pan. On waxed paper, stir together the flour, baking soda, cream of tartar, and salt. In a large bowl, cream the butter, sugar, and vanilla extract. Beat in eggs, then add the flour mixture until blended. With a wide spatula, spread batter over bottom and sides of pan. Refrigerate.
Cream cheese filling: Without washing the beater, beat all ingredients together.
Custard topping: Without washing the beater, beat all ingredients together.
Apple topping: Pare and core the apples. Cut into 1/8 to 1/4-inch slices. Remove the pan from the refrigerator; spread cream cheese filling over bottom. Arrange apples over filling; pour custard topping over apples. Sprinkle with pecans. Bake on a rack below center of oven for 1 hour. Cover top with foil and continue baking 50-60 minutes more until custard is set. Place on rack to cool. Remove foil; loosen sides; remove the band.
